title: Update working memory from Edge disabled sync auth session
summary: Durable context learned while fixing Google Drive sync startup auth checks for disabled sync mode on Edge.

claims.text: Drive sync uses `chrome.identity.getAuthToken` when available, persists access tokens with expiry in extension local storage, and falls back to `chrome.identity.launchWebAuthFlow` for interactive auth when needed; constructor-driven `loadState()` now performs its non-interactive auth-status lookup only when the stored sync mode is not `disabled`, leaving disabled mode unauthenticated without touching identity APIs.

When Google Drive sync mode is `disabled`, startup state loading must not call `chrome.identity.getAuthToken`; issue #538 established that Microsoft Edge may expose that function but throw an unsupported-API error when it is invoked.

For non-disabled sync modes, `GoogleDriveSyncService.loadState()` still calls `getAuthToken(false)` and sets `state.isAuthenticated` from the returned token, so enabled sync modes keep the startup auth-status check.
The Google Drive sync token-persistence test explicitly mocks local storage with `gvSyncMode: 'auto'` because the default disabled mode no longer exercises the startup `getAuthToken(false)` path.

metadata.reason: The historical session identified issue #538 as an Edge-specific failure where disabled sync still triggered `getAuthToken(false)` during `loadState()`, and the accepted fix was to skip that auth check when the mode is `disabled`.
